capabilities.note: Strong structural attestations: risk_level, charter_v7_checked, prev_receipt_hash, signatures, 6-key summaries.

capabilities.capability: Source-citation governance (every number cites its lineage)
capabilities.note: Receipts strong, but content lineage (GL lines, ERP txns, assumptions) is not first-class in the envelope nor queryable in Atrium.

capabilities.capability: Tiered approval gates by materiality/audience (board vs internal)
capabilities.note: HumanApprovalGate/breath_gate_token exist but binary — not tiered by materiality/audience as Ch 10 demands.
Not yet a first-class receipted primitive; strong on policy-as-code, light on versioned methodology artifacts.
prev_receipt_hash + 6-key summary + charter_v7_checked in compliance_engine + node_api/server.py.
Maps to ContextAdapter + operator-defined roles, but provenance/freshness/strategic-role metadata not visible in Atrium.
Breath-gates exist but lack the book's visible 5-level calibration and override-rate feedback.
Not first-class envelope/receipt sections.
Not yet a first-class, receipted operation.
role_spec + BoundRole + ContextAdapter implement governed roles (allowed_action_classes, policy-as-code).
Implementation is technical; lacks task-level Human/Agent classification with accountability attribution.
No first-class trust-level/promotion/team-matrix surface in Atrium or node_api.

capabilities.capability: Structured Agent Brief (5-element delegation) as receipted artifact
capabilities.note: Excellent operationalization but not yet a native receipted artifact type.
Override/rollback + alignment to authority matrices supported via breath-gates.
Strong extension of the Role Matrix, but role_spec + Atrium lack supply-chain layered visibility / Ops Cockpit.
No first-class persistent monitoring agent/gate; receipts primarily reactive.
Not yet native, reusable receipted governance patterns.

capabilities.capability: Human gate before any employment action
capabilities.note: HumanApprovalGate exists but not configurable for HR thresholds / qualified-reviewer roles / 'reviewed before employment action' attribution.
Strongest ethical language in the series, but no first-class bias-auditing primitive.

capabilities.capability: Individual transparency (data-subject access to own data)
capabilities.note: Harness excels at operator/admin receipts; no native controlled access for data subjects (employees).
No first-class 'uncomfortable truths reach decision-makers, no filtering' enforcement.
Book describes almost exactly what exists: AuditRecord, prev_receipt_hash, 6-key summary, charter_v7_checked, Merkle receipts.
Internal receipts strong; the external-proof verification-packet export + simple public-ledger verification UX not yet first-class/polished.

capabilities.capability: Qualified-reviewer gates (e.g. only BSA officer approves SARs) + attribution
capabilities.note: Gates exist but not easily role-configurable for high-stakes actions with explicit receipt attribution.
Ingestion layer that keeps policies updated from external sources is still thin.

capabilities.capability: K1 human judgment on pricing + customer-facing actions
capabilities.note: Human-in-loop on high-stakes pricing/deal decisions central + non-delegable; operator-defined governance.

capabilities.capability: Progressive review gates + batch limits + suppression checks for outbound
capabilities.note: Only partially present/configurable in role_spec + breath-gates (test-batch gates, batch limits, opt-out checks).
Win-rate vs margin vs gross-profit optimization config with approval chains is not first-class.
No clean cockpit for behavioral forecasts, pricing chains, accuracy trends (CRO + CFO).

capabilities.capability: K1 human judgment on safety-critical decisions
capabilities.note: Human judgment central + operator-defined roles with hard constraints (safety as a governance requirement).

capabilities.capability: Architecturally non-overridable safety limits (LOTO / E-stop / zones)
capabilities.note: Hard constraints partially expressible; not yet architecturally non-overridable with receipt attribution.

capabilities.capability: Always-on Safety Monitoring companion + dedicated Atrium view
capabilities.note: No persistent always-on safety monitoring infra or non-dismissible Atrium view.

capabilities.capability: SQDC evaluation lens + progressive threshold tightening
capabilities.note: Not first-class configurable/auditable (SQDC dashboard; 'turn the screws' threshold history).
Maps almost perfectly to receipting (AuditRecord + prev_receipt_hash + 6-key), breath-gates, and role binding.

capabilities.capability: Composable 4-agent ARET loop as a reusable, versionable template
capabilities.note: Pattern is proven but not yet a first-class reusable template with built-in propose-approve-execute + receipts.
No first-class cross-agent state / pending-proposal / constitutional-compliance surface.

capabilities.capability: Component registry for reusable multi-agent patterns
capabilities.note: 'Best use case is the reuse case' — no component registry yet.
Visible in the demo + compliance joint-attestation logic; not yet a first-class principal-boundary primitive with Atrium mandate switching.

capabilities.capability: Runtime-Exercise verify vs real memory shape (Merkle, not .actions[])
capabilities.note: Book v1.3 handed jq '.actions[-1].action_class' but memory is Merkle (core.py:292) + per-demo *_summary.json; v1.4 reconciled the language (was true DRIFT).

capabilities.capability: First-class bind_role action class on node.load_role()
capabilities.note: Promotion candidate: binding emits no governance action class today.

capabilities.capability: Cross-role review / veto as a first-class joint-receipt primitive

Largely manual / policy-as-code rather than harness-enforced during diligence.

capabilities.capability: Thin-waist node_api + starter-first architecture (Atrium as replaceable lens)
